behavior_summary: U8P_GRAVE_EW::look is the object's inspect/U8P_GRAVE_EW::look handler: when the player examines the gravestone it displays a short bark text ("gravestone ") and then yields/suspends.
U8P_GRAVE_EW::use is the object's U8P_GRAVE_EW::use handler and does not implement local logic; it delegates the interaction to a shared handler GRAVE_NS::func00CB and then yields/suspends.
No global flags are read or written by the code in this excerpt; no local data or branching logic is present.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRAVE_EW::look: linear sequence — emit a single bark string then suspend/yield the process.
U8P_GRAVE_EW::use: single external call to GRAVE_NS::func00CB then suspend/yield; the process itself does not perform branching or flag manipulation.
open_questions: What specific behavior does GRAVE_NS::func00CB implement for the gravestone 'U8P_GRAVE_EW::use' action (e.g., open tomb, start quest, give item, dialogue)?
Does the gravestone instance provide any context/this-pointer or parameters to GRAVE_NS::func00CB (the excerpt shows a call but not the forwarded arguments)?
Is the trailing space in the bark text "gravestone " intentional (formatting artifact) or meaningful in composed UI text?
